one_liner: Sentry provides a broad, well-documented REST API with self-serve bearer-token (and OAuth2) authentication, plus an official hosted MCP server, making it readily buildable for AI-agent tooling today.

auth_methods.notes: Bearer auth tokens: user auth tokens (personal) or organization auth tokens (org-scoped, prefix sntrys_). Passed via Authorization: Bearer header. Scoped permissions (project:read, org:admin, etc).
Supported for third-party applications integrating with Sentry, per search result [3].

self_serve_notes: Free Developer plan (5,000 errors/10,000 transactions per month, single user) requires no credit card and allows self-serve creation of user or org auth tokens via account/org settings. Paid plans (Team/Business) needed for higher quota or multi-user orgs, but API access itself is not gated behind sales.

api_surface.notes: Sentry's REST API (api/0) covers organizations, projects, issues, events, teams, members, releases, etc. Full OpenAPI schema available (getsentry/sentry-api-schema).

evidence.supports: REST API reference with broad endpoint coverage
Bearer token auth, user and organization auth tokens, scopes, error responses
Self-serve creation of auth tokens via User Settings / Organization Settings, scope reference
Free Developer plan details, no credit card required, quota limits
Official remote MCP server URL supporting OAuth
